[Affordability Confirmation] To Devin Burge,

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is funded. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be canceled. Receiving money based on false representations made during an application can leave you exposed to litigation for misrepresentation and fraud, which is a felony under US Law.

interactions.body: (Borrower GT Rule 1 SMS) Hi Kelley, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, so we should too! They can be ANYONE who has a 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Homeowners with slightly lower scores will also have a good chance of approval. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! Find tips here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/8OJLWFLH - we'd love to add you to the 40,000 customers we have already helped like you. If you still can't find a cosigner, please reply CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Ben, Transform Credit

If you still can't find a cosigner, please reply CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Ben, Transform Credit

interactions.body: (Borrower GT Rule 1 SMS) Hi Anne, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, and we should too! They can be ANYONE who is a US resident, has 750+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! You'll find tips here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/BO4IE8R5 - we'd love your loan to add to the +$100 million we've already paid out. If you still can't find a cosigner, please respond CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Jade, Transform Credit

[Affordability Confirmation] To James Bolden,

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is funded. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be canceled. Receiving money based on false representations made during an application can leave you exposed to litigation for misrepresentation and fraud, which is a felony under US Law.

interactions.body: (Borrower GT Rule 1 SMS) Hi James, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, so we should too! They can be ANYONE who has a 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Homeowners with slightly lower scores will also have a good chance of approval. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! Find tips here: https://togetherloans.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/U1NKICAL - we'd love to add you to the 40,000 customers we have already helped like you. If you still can't find a cosigner, please reply CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Ben, Together Loans

interactions.body: (Borrower GT Rule 1 SMS) Hi Jessica, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, so we should too! They can be ANYONE who has a 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Homeowners with slightly lower scores will also have a good chance of approval. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! Find tips here: https://togetherloans.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/I4TE88OB - we'd love to add you to the 40,000 customers we have already helped like you. If you still can't find a cosigner, please reply CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Ben, Together Loans

[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0600293213080201130828] Hi Ronald,
 
Thanks for answering. 

When you applied for a loan with us, we offered two different products: Loans up to $7000 with a co-signer, and CreditBuilder which is a small loan held on our customer's behalf that customers make monthly payments against, building up their credit score. 

I can see that you signed up for a Transform CreditBuilder account when applying for a loan of $3250, on March 24th, 2023. We sent you a ‘Welcome to Credit Builder’ email on sign-up, confirming your account, how it works, and how you can manage it.

Your CreditBuilder helps build your credit, even if you do not borrow from us. When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 24 months. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as interest against a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the 24 months have passed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, CreditBuilder is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

You can find out more about CreditBuilder here: transformcredit.com/transform-credit-builder/ However, I can see that your CreditBuilder account is actually canceled, so no further payments will be taken. If you wish to set-up a new CreditBuilder account, you can do so here https://rgct.io/s/Y6MOT74L. CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. 

When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, Transform Credit can still help you access funds of up to $7000 too! At which point, the CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.

If you need help, please let me know.
 
Many thanks,
Michelle, TransformCredit

interactions.body: ('Call me back' BRW VM) Hi Marcellus, thanks for your call. None of our agents are available, please reply here with your question & we'll get back to you. Thanks, Transform Credit

(Requesting lower loan without a GT SMS) Hi Marcellus, Transform Credit requires a cosigner, regardless of the requested loan amount. A cosigner shows us that someone trusts you and that we should too, regardless of your credit score. A suitable cosigner is someone who has a very good credit history with no trouble paying bills in the past, and who can afford to make the monthly payments if you don't. Although they don't have to be a homeowner, it may increase the possibility of us accepting them as your cosigner if they do own their own home. If you need to update your loan amount or term, you can do so here: https://tcrdt.com/s/IM1CX26E Thanks, TransformCredit

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The Information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is paid out. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be cancelled. Receiving money based on false representations made during an application can leave you exposed to litigation for misrepresentation and fraud, which is a felony under US Law.

IF THE INFORMATION BELOW IS CORRECT, YOU DO NOT NEED TO REPLY TO THIS EMAIL.

Information provided to Transform Credit by you:

Borrower: Berry Smith
IP: 12.75.36.123

Rent: $1048.00

Other Expenses: $1500.00
Your Credit Commitments: $0.00
Transform Credit Repayment: $185.89
To be safe: $50.00
Total available after expenditure and this loan repayment: $3266.11


Yours sincerely
Transform credit Applications Dept

interactions.body:
(Progress Update - LEAD/NoGT BRW Email) [RE:Affordability Confirmation] Hi Rachel,

We've got your $5000 reserved, you and your cosigner just need to finish your application by submitting your information.

Use this link to pick up where you left off: https://tcrdt.com/s/KCFC06M4

We will always do everything we can to make sure your loan is paid out as quickly as possible. We aim to pay out every loan within 24 hours of the cosigner being accepted (remember - your cosigner can be ANYONE you know who is a US resident, has a 750+ credit score and good history of paying their bills on time). 

If you and your cosigner complete the application online and all checks are completed without issue, we could get the money to you sooner than 24 hours. If we find that we need more information when we review your application, we'll be in contact - so make sure you're available to speak to us & follow any instructions that we send you as soon as possible.

In the meantime, you'll find helpful answers to lots of questions in our website FAQs: https://transformcredit.com/faq

Jade,
Transform Credit

interactions.body: (AI BW GTQs - Secure the loan with collateral SMS) Hi Jonathan, we offer unsecured loans and therefore, sadly, we can't accept car titles or home deeds as collateral. Our loans are based on trust. A cosigner shows us that someone trusts you to repay your loan, and that we should too! This trust allows us to lend to you even if you've been turned down elsewhere. If you don't have a suitable cosigner, you can reply CANCEL. Or use this link to continue when you're ready: https://tglns.com/s/XTF82U7W Thanks, Together Loans

interactions.body: (Borrower GT Rule 1 SMS) Hi Jonathan, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, so we should too! They can be ANYONE who has a 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Homeowners with slightly lower scores will also have a good chance of approval. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! Find tips here: https://togetherloans.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tglns.com/s/J1LSUAPK - we'd love to add you to the 40,000 customers we have already helped like you. If you still can't find a cosigner, please reply CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Ben, Together Loans

(Appeals Auto Response BRW Email) [RE:Missing Subject] Hi Jonathan, 

Thanks for contacting Appeals. 

We understand that it can be disheartening when you're struggling to find a suitable cosigner, don't worry though - the most important thing is we've already accepted your application and want to help you.

There are a few reasons we could find someone to be unsuitable - the most common being a less than perfect credit history. It will be up to your cosigner to make any loan payments that you do not, looking at their Experian credit history allows us to check that they can manage this in addition to their existing commitments. If a potential cosigner has missed or late payments, we can't be certain they'd be able to afford the loan payments while maintaining their other accounts. Alternatively, If your potential cosigner's credit file is locked, we won't have been able to confirm either way (they can apply as soon as their credit file is unlocked: https://tcrdt.com/s/S0ESE7PS). 

When you're considering who to ask, remember your cosigner must have a 750+ credit score and good history of paying their bills on time (homeowners and mortgage payers are more likely to be accepted). It's important to remember that your cosigner can only be on one loan with Together Loans at a time too.

If you've provided someone who is unsure why they weren't accepted, we recommend they check their Experian credit report/score for free at: https://www.experian.com/.

In the meantime, the $3000 will still be reserved for you. We'd love for your loan to add to the $100 million we've already paid out to people just like you. Give this link to your cosigner to register online: https://tcrdt.com/s/S0ESE7PS and feel free to check out our FAQs for further help: https://https://togetherloans.com/faq.

Jade@Together Loans

P.S. I am ready to pay $3000 out within 24 hours of your cosigner being approved, but if you don't plan to provide a new cosigner please let me know by replying CANCEL so I can reserve it for someone else

Dear Amber,

interactions.body: (Borrower GT Rule 1 SMS) Hi Vickie, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, so we should too! They can be ANYONE who has a 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Homeowners with slightly lower scores will also have a good chance of approval. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! Find tips here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/8PNI6TKT - we'd love to add you to the 40,000 customers we have already helped like you. If you still can't find a cosigner, please reply CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Ben, Transform Credit

interactions.body: (2FA code issues BRW SMS) Hi Joseph. As a Transform Credit customer, we send you lots of important information via SMS. It's really important that we have a cell number for you, and your cosigner separately, so we can keep you both up to date with crucial information! If the code is incorrect, request a new code and enter as soon as you receive it (the code expires after a short time). If delivery of your code to your cell is taking a long time, or you're not receiving it at all, try restarting your phone to refresh network connectivity and make sure you're in an area with strong coverage. Jade, Transform Credit

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The Information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. interactions.body:
(AI BW GTQs - GT requirements Email) [RE: Last day for FastTrack] Hi Jacob,

A cosigner should be someone who trusts you and agrees to make any repayments you don't. Most of our customers ask a close friend or family member to be their cosigner.

Suitable cosigners should have a good history of paying their bills on time. Homeowners don't usually need to have a score as high as 750 and are more likely to be accepted with a lower score, as long as they pay their bills on time. Generally anyone with over a 700 score has a fair chance of being accepted as long as they have a good payment history.

If you are unsure whether your cosigner will be accepted, we recommend asking them to complete their application online, where they will be informed if they are accepted or not. If you need to provide a new cosigner, please use this link: https://tcrdt.com/s/P1VHX0J9

Thanks,
Transform Credit

(Borrower GT Rule 1 Email) [RE:Affordability Confirmation] Hi Sara Maria, 

Thanks for reaching out, we understand that it can seem difficult to find a cosigner at first. Don't worry though, we help people like you every day!

We're a cosigner lender, all of our customers must have a cosigner for us to lend to them. A cosigner is a great sign that someone trusts you, and that we should too regardless of your credit history! If your cosigner trusts you, then we will.

A cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor or even a colleague) who is a US resident, has a 700+ credit score and good history of paying their bills on time - someone who owns their home or pays a mortgage is much more likely to be accepted. Remember, as long as you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to make a payment! You might find some useful tips on how to approach finding or speaking to a potential cosigner here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans

When you have found someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/1X3KHERN - we'd love for your loan to add to the $100 million we've already paid out to people just like you. If you still find that you're not able to find a cosigner, please respond to this email with CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else.

Thanks, 
Jade@Transform Credit

interactions.body:
(CB CANCEL - Specific email subject lines) [RE:CreditBuilder Payment Reminder] Hi Sara Maris,
 
Thanks for getting in touch.
 
I can see that you signed up for a CreditBuilder account AND applied for a $3000 loan on 02/03/2026. 

When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 1-2 years. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as payment for a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the loan term is completed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, your CreditBuilder account is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, Together Loans can help you access funds too! At which point, any CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.

You can find out more about the Together Loans CreditBuilder here - https://togetherloans.com/together-loans-credit-builder/ . However, I can confirm that I have canceled your CreditBuilder account, you will not be charged again. 

We still have your loan application approved for you. All you need is a cosigner, a friend or a family member who has a 750+ credit score and agrees to make the payment if you can't. Follow this link to pick up where you left off: https://tcrdt.com/s/OMT0ZQYX

If you need help, please let me know
 
Have a great day, 
Jade @ Together Loans CreditBuilder

[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0605001009241409010620] Hi Ignacia,
 
I appreciate our loans may seem expensive, but in context, our interest rate is not high at all. Lending to customers who have bad credit or no credit at all is more risky and typically if you have a damaged credit score or no credit at all, there is no other option than pay-day lenders whose interest rate can be in the hundreds of percent. 
 
We offer a more flexible loan option and we don’t believe in hidden fees or charges. Additionally, a lot of our customers pay off the loans early when their financial situation improves, which saves them a lot of money in interest because our interest is calculated daily so you only pay for the time you borrow. You can settle early to save on interest, without the penalties that other companies charge, if you chose to do this.   

Please use this link to continue with your application: https://tcrdt.com/s/JYALX9ZX
 
Many thanks, Transform Credit

[Affordability Confirmation] To Ignacia Cortez,

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The Information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is paid out. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be cancelled. Receiving money based on false representations made during an application can leave you exposed to litigation for misrepresentation and fraud, which is a felony under US Law.

Hi Heedy,

Thank you for letting me know. I understand the situation with Steven's work hours and the shared bank account. Unfortunately, our policy requires the borrower and co-signer to have separate bank accounts for security and compliance reasons.

interactions.body: (Borrower GT Rule 1 SMS) Hi James, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, so we should too! They can be ANYONE who has a 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Homeowners with slightly lower scores will also have a good chance of approval. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! Find tips here: https://togetherloans.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/APK3KCP6 - we'd love to add you to the 40,000 customers we have already helped like you. If you still can't find a cosigner, please reply CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Ben, Together Loans

(Want to speak to someone - PreVC BRW Email) [RE:A message from Together Loans] Hi James,

Thanks for reaching out to us, we're more than happy to help you with any queries and questions that you have. 

We do have a phone number, +1 470 435 6300, this goes to our 24/7 interactive call handler. This service will give you live updates about your application, and how it is progressing through to payout! Calling this number allows us to give you instant information and saves you long waits to speak to a person.

We also have a really helpful section on our website with answers to our frequently asked questions: https://togetherloans.com/Help/Contact

If you question isn't answered by either of these services, you're welcome to reply to this email explaining your problem in detail and someone will get back to you as soon as possible. 

Thanks,
Jade@Together Loans

interactions.body:
[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0610140135092823051700] Hi Magali,

Thank you for reaching out.

When you applied for a loan with us, we offered two different products: Loans up to $7000 with a co-signer, and CreditBuilder which is a small loan held on our customer's behalf that customers make monthly payments against, building up their credit score.

The CreditBuilder is offered to everyone that applies for a loan with us, and our customers will have the choice to sign up or not sign up for the CreditBuilder loan, as part of the application process. I can see that on June 28th, when you applied for this loan of $6000, you also reached the page where the CreditBuilder product was explained and offered, and you chose to sign up when clicked on “ok, set up my account”. I've attached a screenshot of this page to this email. 

After signing up, we immediately sent you a “Welcome to Transform CreditBuilder!” email, confirming your account, and further explaining the benefits of the product you had opted into. I can see this was sent to you at June 28th to magalifdz@gmail.com. The email also provided you with your login link, so you could access your account and manage/cancel it, at any time, even before the first payment.

When you signed up for CreditBuilder, you took the first step in building your score. Each month you pay $5, we report these payments to the Credit Bureaus as interest against the loan that has been held on your behalf. These successful payments build your credit history, helping to improve your score. The great thing about CreditBuilder is that if you decide to cancel/stop paying, CreditBuilder is simply marked as paid in full and so missed payments can never undo the positive impact that your on-time payments have been making on your credit score. Our customers use CreditBuilder as a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. 

We also sent you a payment reminder before collecting your CreditBuilder payment, to your email, reminding you that your $5 payment was due. This again provided you with your login details, where you were able to manage or cancel your CreditBuilder, should you have decided you no longer wanted to benefit from the product.

Based on the above, we believe we acted in line with your instructions. However, we now understand that you may not have intentionally signed-up to CreditBuilder, and, so as a gesture of goodwill, we have issued you a refund to put you back in the position you were in, before opening your CreditBuilder account. 

We will also close your account, meaning we will not attempt any further payments.

We understand that confusion can sometimes arise and we appreciate your patience as we work to rectify this situation. Please allow 3 business days for the refund to show in your account.  

Many thanks,
Denzel@TransformCredit

[jennifertaylor] spk_0: Your call has been forwarded to an automatic voice message system. 918-837-1921 is not available. Please record your message. When you have finished recording, you may hang up or press 1 for more options.
spk_1: Hi Madison, this is Jennifer with Together Loans. I'm just Calling because of your message that you sent us about your Bank account and your routing number. We do verify that information over the phone to make sure that we have the correct information in our system that was put in. We also need to get your card information and go over the verbal contract with you. If you can, when you get a moment, please give us a call back at 470-435-6300. Thank you.

[Your Together Loans Loan Application : 0625162203070510011011] Hi Madison, We do ask for you to confirm your banking information over the phone when we are going over the final details for your application so we can be sure we have your accurate information. We will also ask for your debit or credit card information at the same time as we use this for identity verification and as a back up payment method. Please call us at 470-435-6300 when you have a moment so we can discuss all of this with you.
Thanks. Together Loans

interactions.body:
[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0601000303092200232621] Hi Spencer Spencer,

Unfortunately, online banks are less likely to be accepted since we are unable to verify the identity of our customers through these companies.

When the loan is ready to be paid out, the $3250 will be deposited into your cosigner's bank account for them to give to you, so it's important we have these details. 

We do this to be absolutely sure that the cosigner is aware of the loan. Additionally, because the cosigner agrees to make any payments that you don't, we can use these details as a backup method of payment if the account goes past due. 

Unfortunately, they will not be able to move forward with their cosigner application without this information. Would you like to cancel their application as a cosigner so you can add a different cosigner?

Thanks, Rebekah@TransformCredit

interactions.body: Hi Spencer, Please send in a picture of your ID (front and back) along with a selfie, and a selfie video with your ID and confirming you are applying for the loan with Together Loans. Also please send in Proof of Address that is on your application, proof must have your name, DOB and connection to the address/state. It can be a pay stub, rent/lease agreement, utility bill, phone bill, or a bank statement (no online banks, must be brick and mortar). The docs must be either PDF or if its a print out then all 4 corners included in the picture along w/ all the required info. You can reply to the text or send an email to hello@togetherloans.com Thanks, Together Loans

[Affordability Confirmation] To Anthony Casias,

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The Information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is paid out. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be cancelled. Receiving money based on false representations made during an application can leave you exposed to charges of criminal fraud, which is a felony under US Law.

interactions.body: Hi Anthony,I apologize for the delay in getting back to you — we’ve been working through a high volume of messages from clients.I see that you set up a Secondary Promise for 8/12; however, this was created after we had already collected the payment from your cosigner. Additionally, a Secondary Promise is considered an informal notice, which means we may still contact your cosigner to make a payment. We have a separate agreement with them to cover the payment if you are unable to make it on the due date.To help avoid this situation in the future, please let us know if a different due date would work better for you? Additionally, if you are able to make a payment on the account, once cleared, we would be able to process a refund to Nestor, if you can do that, please use this link: https://tcrdt.com/s/CNVD7D3A. Transform Credit

Hi Anthony, I have attempted to speak to you as per your request - however, I was directed to your voicemail. Once eligible you can borrow more funds through our Top Up program. A 'top-up' refers to borrowing additional funds. You can borrow up to $8,500 (depending on your state); a portion will be used to pay off your current loan, and the remaining amount will be deposited into your co-signer's bank account, just like your other loan with us. This option is available only to customers with a strong history of timely and complete payments and can be accessed exclusively through the app https://tglns.com/s/60KPELWZ. At this time, you are not eligible however you can check your eligibility through the app. If you have further questions, can you please confirm a suitable time to give you a callback, or call us back at (916) 999-5799 opt 2? Thanks, Together Loans

spk_1: Hi Carolina, this is Joe Calling from transform-credit. Just, uh, saw that you had a couple questions and I wanted to kind of give you an update on where you are on this loan process so you can, um, you can, uh, finish up your end. Um, you can give us a call back at 470-435-6300. We look forward to speaking.

Hi Carolina, 
These are unsecured loans. The interest rate on this loan is 35.99%. I appreciate our loans may seem expensive, but in context, our interest rate is not high at all. Lending to customers who have bad credit or no credit at all is more risky and typically if you have a damaged credit score or no credit at all, there is no other option than pay-day lenders whose interest rate can be in the hundreds of percent. We offer a more flexible loan option and we don’t believe in hidden fees or charges. Additionally, a lot of our customers pay off the loans early when their financial situation improves, which saves them a lot of money in interest because our interest is calculated daily so you only pay for the time you borrow. You can settle early to save on interest, without the penalties that other companies charge, if you chose to do this. 

Thanks, TransformCredit
